name: Spaghetti Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Do not increase the amount of onion, peppers, or mushrooms.
2. Wash the tomatoes and dip them in boiling water for 30-60 seconds until the skins split. Remove from hot water and peel off the skins. Core and quarter the tomatoes.
3. Boil the tomatoes uncovered in a large saucepan for about 20 minutes.
4. Press through a food mill or sieve.
5. Heat vegetable oil in a pan, sauté onion, garlic, celery, and mushrooms until tender.
6. Combine the sauteed vegetables with the tomatoes and remaining spices (salt, pepper, oregano, parsley), and brown sugar.
7. Bring to a boil and simmer uncovered, stirring frequently, until thick enough for serving. The volume will reduce by nearly half.
8. Pour into hot jars, leaving 1-inch headspace. Wipe jar rims.
9. Adjust lids and process in a pressure canner at 10-11 pounds of pressure for 20 minutes (pints) or 25 minutes (quarts).
